name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Non-Think)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (284B total / 13B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention
note: Smaller, cheaper sibling MoE released alongside V4-Pro on the same day; fast default mode. (fast/low-latency mode; the default tier a user gets without selecting anything).

results.notes: Terminal Bench 2.0 (Acc.), Non-Think mode, Table 7 of the official 'DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence' technical report (arXiv:2606.19348)
SWE-Pro (Resolved), Non-Think mode, Table 7 of the DeepSeek-V4 technical report

verificationNotes:
Confirmed via official DeepSeek api-docs news post and the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash HF model card (284B total/13B active, MIT, 1M context, same three-tier benchmark table format as V4-Pro).

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) for DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Non-Think) against (1) the official deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash HF model card, which fetched cleanly and shows the full "Instruct Model - Reasoning Modes" / "Comparison Across Modes" table (Non-Think vs Think High vs Think Max columns) — its complete row list is MMLU-Pro, SimpleQA-Verified, Chinese-SimpleQA, GPQA Diamond, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces, HMMT 2026 Feb, IMOAnswerBench, Apex, Apex Shortlist, MRCR 1M, CorpusQA 1M, Terminal Bench 2.0, SWE Verified, SWE Pro, SWE Multilingual, BrowseComp, HLE w/ tools, MCPAtlas, GDPval-AA, Toolathlon — none of which are mmlu (plain), math, aime, humaneval, or mmmu. The model card's separate "Base Model Evaluation Results" section does report MATH (57.4 EM) and HumanEval (69.5 Pass@1), but those are for the DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base pretrained checkpoint, a distinct artifact from this file's Non-Think instruct configuration, so they were not attributed here. (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis' deepseek-v4-flash-non-reasoning page confirms via its live Intelligence Breakdown that its current v4.1 index (GDPval-AA, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench, SciCode, HLE, GPQA, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, IFBench, MMMU-Pro) does not include plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, or MMMU either; Epoch AI's benchmarking hub returned no page for this model; Vals.ai's models directory lists DeepSeek V4 Flash but has no benchmark-score table for it. (3) Sibling-model cross-reference: deepseek-v4-flash-think-high.json and deepseek-v4-pro-non-think.json — both released the same day from the same official table — likewise carry no mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/mmmu entries, confirming this is a consistent absence across the whole DeepSeek-V4 launch, not a one-off gap. Additionally mmmu is categorically inapplicable: this model's modalities are text-only (vision: false), so it was never evaluated on a vision benchmark. Conclusion: DeepSeek never published mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/mmmu scores for this specific Non-Think instruct configuration; all five gaps are a legitimate absence (consistent with frontier labs skipping saturated/legacy benchmarks in favor of mmlu-pro, HMMT, IMOAnswerBench, etc.), not a research failure — omitted rather than estimated.

2026-07-18: Re-chased the flagged 'mmmu' gap for DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Non-Think). Confirmed architecturally inapplicable, not merely unpublished: (1) this file's own modalities field is ["text"] and capabilities.vision is false; (2) independent web search corroborates DeepSeek-V4 (Flash and Pro) shipped as a text-only MoE — multiple sources (jocheojeda.com's "The Vision Model That Wasn't There: DeepSeek V4" post-mortem, and general V4 coverage) confirm multimodal/vision support was rumored/planned but never shipped, with explicit note that "as of late June 2026, there is no DeepSeek model on any endpoint that accepts an image"; DeepSeek's actual vision-language models (DeepSeek-VL, DeepSeek-VL2) are separate, older model families unrelated to the V4 line and not applicable to this corpus entry; (3) no independent tracker (Artificial Analysis' deepseek-v4-flash page, BenchLM, llm-stats.com) lists a plain MMMU score for any V4-Flash variant — AA's intelligence-index breakdown for this model includes MMMU-Pro only for vision-capable peers, not this text-only model, consistent with the prior 2026-07-16 audit. Conclusion unchanged and reinforced: MMMU is architecturally inapplicable for this text-only model, not a research gap. No new benchmark results were added this pass.
